content: Hey, I'm Alice. I'm working on a new project called Project Alpha — it's a network of sensor nodes that capture camera and audio from around my house. I want to feed the data into my memory system.

content.text: Nice — Project Alpha sounds like an ambitious physical-world memory capture system. What's your current hardware setup for the sensor nodes?

content: Three nodes so far: office, kitchen, bedroom. Each one has a single-board computer with a 12MP wide-angle camera and an audio HAT. I swapped from the previous camera last week because the field of view was too narrow.

content.text: That's a solid stack. The wide-angle lens should give you much better coverage than the previous camera. Are the nodes streaming continuously or motion-triggered?

content: Motion-triggered right now. I'm using a daemon.py that runs lores 320x240 motion detection and kicks into 1280x720 @ 15fps MJPEG when it sees movement. Audio HAT picks up voice too.

content.text: Smart — that's a good bandwidth/quality tradeoff for a low-power board. Where are you storing the captured clips?

content: On a local storage server with plenty of space. Next step is running the clips through a dedicated inference server.

content.text: Got it. A local NAS + dedicated GPU box is the right architecture for this. What model are you running on the GPU side?

content: Qwen 2.5 7B via Ollama for now. I want to swap to a multimodal model soon for the vision side. Also — correction on what I said earlier: I prefer bun over npm now, I switched last month. All my new projects use bun.

content.text: Noted on the bun preference. Qwen 2.5 7B is a solid starting point — for multimodal you might look at Qwen2-VL or LLaVA-Next.

content: I decided to use TypeScript for the frontend — I tried JavaScript first but the type safety is worth it for this kind of project. React 18 + Vite + Tauri 2.0.

content.text: That's the right stack for a native-feeling desktop app with a modern frontend. Tauri 2.0 is much nicer than 1.x.

content: ok thanks, let me go grab coffee
